testcase.name: runScenario["User must be able to add the selected product to the cart using Add Cart button.", "User must be able to add the selected product to the cart"](1)

failure.message: Expected condition failed: waiting for visibility of all 0 elements, but no elements were found (tried for 20 seconds with 500 milliseconds interval) Build info: version: '4.43.0', revision: 'dd0f534' System info: os.name: 'Windows 11', os.arch: 'amd64', os.version: '10.0', java.version: '25.0.1' Driver info: org.openqa.selenium.chrome.ChromeDriver Capabilities {acceptInsecureCerts: false, browserName: chrome, browserVersion: 149.0.7827.54, chrome: {chromedriverVersion: 149.0.7827.155 (07b52360cc1..., userDataDir: C:\Users\Lenovo\AppData\Loc...}, fedcm:accounts: true, goog:chromeOptions: {debuggerAddress: localhost:65492}, goog:processID: 21300, networkConnectionEnabled: false, pageLoadStrategy: normal, platformName: windows, proxy: Proxy(), se:cdp: ws://localhost:65492/devtoo..., se:cdpVersion: 149.0.7827.54, setWindowRect: true, strictFileInteractability: false, timeouts: {implicit: 0, pageLoad: 300000, script: 30000}, unhandledPromptBehavior: dismiss and notify, webauthn:extension:credBlob: true, webauthn:extension:largeBlob: true, webauthn:extension:minPinLength: true, webauthn:extension:prf: true, webauthn:virtualAuthenticators: true} Session ID: a9fa3a03746fb4a0b6b24442f7d034a5

testcase.failure: org.openqa.selenium.TimeoutException: Expected condition failed: waiting for visibility of all 0 elements, but no elements were found (tried for 20 seconds with 500 milliseconds interval) Build info: version: '4.43.0', revision: 'dd0f534' System info: os.name: 'Windows 11', os.arch: 'amd64', os.version: '10.0', java.version: '25.0.1' Driver info: org.openqa.selenium.chrome.ChromeDriver Capabilities {acceptInsecureCerts: false, browserName: chrome, browserVersion: 149.0.7827.54, chrome: {chromedriverVersion: 149.0.7827.155 (07b52360cc1..., userDataDir: C:\Users\Lenovo\AppData\Loc...}, fedcm:accounts: true, goog:chromeOptions: {debuggerAddress: localhost:65492}, goog:processID: 21300, networkConnectionEnabled: false, pageLoadStrategy: normal, platformName: windows, proxy: Proxy(), se:cdp: ws://localhost:65492/devtoo..., se:cdpVersion: 149.0.7827.54, setWindowRect: true, strictFileInteractability: false, timeouts: {implicit: 0, pageLoad: 300000, script: 30000}, unhandledPromptBehavior: dismiss and notify, webauthn:extension:credBlob: true, webauthn:extension:largeBlob: true, webauthn:extension:minPinLength: true, webauthn:extension:prf: true, webauthn:virtualAuthenticators: true} Session ID: a9fa3a03746fb4a0b6b24442f7d034a5 at org.openqa.selenium.support.ui.WebDriverWait.timeoutException(WebDriverWait.java:85) at org.openqa.selenium.support.ui.FluentWait.until(FluentWait.java:234) at Pages.AddCart.clickAddCart(AddCart.java:107) at stepDefinitions.AddCartSteps.AddCart(AddCartSteps.java:37) at ✽.User click on Add to cart button of the product titled "Pyjamas Set".(file:///C:/Users/Lenovo/eclipse-workspace/Amazon/src/test/resources/features/AddCart.feature:12)
testcase.system-out:
Scenario: User must be able to add the selected product to the cart using Add Cart button. # src/test/resources/features/AddCart.feature:19 Given User is On Amazon login page. # stepDefinitions.LoginSteps.UseronLoginpage() When user enters a valid user email and pwd. # stepDefinitions.LoginSteps.validemail() Then click on login button. # stepDefinitions.LoginSteps.clkLogin() And User is On Amazon home after succesful login. # stepDefinitions.HomePageSteps.homePage() When User search for product "babyboy". # stepDefinitions.AddCartSteps.searchProduct(java.lang.String) Search box value =babyboy Then User selects the "babyboy dress 2-3 years" appears from the dropdown list. # stepDefinitions.AddCartSteps.chooseProduct(java.lang.String) Then User redirected to results screen. # stepDefinitions.AddCartSteps.ResultScreen() [] And User click on Add to cart button of the product titled "Pyjamas Set". # stepDefinitions.AddCartSteps.AddCart(java.lang.String) org.openqa.selenium.TimeoutException: Expected condition failed: waiting for visibility of all 0 elements, but no elements were found (tried for 20 seconds with 500 milliseconds interval) Build info: version: '4.43.0', revision: 'dd0f534' System info: os.name: 'Windows 11', os.arch: 'amd64', os.version: '10.0', java.version: '25.0.1' Driver info: org.openqa.selenium.chrome.ChromeDriver Capabilities {acceptInsecureCerts: false, browserName: chrome, browserVersion: 149.0.7827.54, chrome: {chromedriverVersion: 149.0.7827.155 (07b52360cc1..., userDataDir: C:\Users\Lenovo\AppData\Loc...}, fedcm:accounts: true, goog:chromeOptions: {debuggerAddress: localhost:65492}, goog:processID: 21300, networkConnectionEnabled: false, pageLoadStrategy: normal, platformName: windows, proxy: Proxy(), se:cdp: ws://localhost:65492/devtoo..., se:cdpVersion: 149.0.7827.54, setWindowRect: true, strictFileInteractability: false, timeouts: {implicit: 0, pageLoad: 300000, script: 30000}, unhandledPromptBehavior: dismiss and notify, webauthn:extension:credBlob: true, webauthn:extension:largeBlob: true, webauthn:extension:minPinLength: true, webauthn:extension:prf: true, webauthn:virtualAuthenticators: true} Session ID: a9fa3a03746fb4a0b6b24442f7d034a5 at org.openqa.selenium.support.ui.WebDriverWait.timeoutException(WebDriverWait.java:85) at org.openqa.selenium.support.ui.FluentWait.until(FluentWait.java:234) at Pages.AddCart.clickAddCart(AddCart.java:107) at stepDefinitions.AddCartSteps.AddCart(AddCartSteps.java:37) at ✽.User click on Add to cart button of the product titled "Pyjamas Set".(file:///C:/Users/Lenovo/eclipse-workspace/Amazon/src/test/resources/features/AddCart.feature:12)
Then examine the cart. # stepDefinitions.AddCartSteps.CInfo() And delete all added products. # stepDefinitions.AddCartSteps.cDelete() And capture the Screenshot # stepDefinitions.Screenshot.takeScreenshot()

testcase.name: runScenario["User must be able to add the selected product to the cart using Add Cart button.", "User must be able to add the selected product to the cart"](2)
testcase.system-out: Scenario: User must be able to add the selected product to the cart using Add Cart button. # src/test/resources/features/AddCart.feature:20 Given User is On Amazon login page. # stepDefinitions.LoginSteps.UseronLoginpage() When user enters a valid user email and pwd. # stepDefinitions.LoginSteps.validemail() Then click on login button. # stepDefinitions.LoginSteps.clkLogin() And User is On Amazon home after succesful login. # stepDefinitions.HomePageSteps.homePage() When User search for product "babyboy". # stepDefinitions.AddCartSteps.searchProduct(java.lang.String) Search box value =babyboy Then User selects the "babyboy dress 2-3 years" appears from the dropdown list. # stepDefinitions.AddCartSteps.chooseProduct(java.lang.String) Then User redirected to results screen. # stepDefinitions.AddCartSteps.ResultScreen() [[[ChromeDriver: chrome on windows (e5b4f811562a860f528a74bfd2e3712b)] -> xpath: //div[@data-component-type='s-search-result'][.//h2//span[contains(text(),'Kuchipoo')]]], [[ChromeDriver: chrome on windows (e5b4f811562a860f528a74bfd2e3712b)] -> xpath: //div[@data-component-type='s-search-result'][.//h2//span[contains(text(),'Kuchipoo')]]], [[ChromeDriver: chrome on windows (e5b4f811562a860f528a74bfd2e3712b)] -> xpath: //div[@data-component-type='s-search-result'][.//h2//span[contains(text(),'Kuchipoo')]]], [[ChromeDriver: chrome on windows (e5b4f811562a860f528a74bfd2e3712b)] -> xpath: //div[@data-component-type='s-search-result'][.//h2//span[contains(text(),'Kuchipoo')]]], [[ChromeDriver: chrome on windows (e5b4f811562a860f528a74bfd2e3712b)] -> xpath: //div[@data-component-type='s-search-result'][.//h2//span[contains(text(),'Kuchipoo')]]], [[ChromeDriver: chrome on windows (e5b4f811562a860f528a74bfd2e3712b)] -> xpath: //div[@data-component-type='s-search-result'][.//h2//span[contains(text(),'Kuchipoo')]]], [[ChromeDriver: chrome on windows (e5b4f811562a860f528a74bfd2e3712b)] -> xpath: //div[@data-component-type='s-search-result'][.//h2//span[contains(text(),'Kuchipoo')]]], [[ChromeDriver: chrome on windows (e5b4f811562a860f528a74bfd2e3712b)] -> xpath: //div[@data-component-type='s-search-result'][.//h2//span[contains(text(),'Kuchipoo')]]]] Products size:8 Product detailsPreviously viewed Kuchipoo Kids Regular Fit Cotton T-Shirts and Pyjamas Set 4.2 (151) Limited time deal ₹597 ₹1,899 (69% off) FREE delivery Tue, 23 Jun See options Parent window ID44B3520FB43CDAF5133E0D5C87A1CFBC Product detailsPreviously viewed Kuchipoo Kids Regular Fit Cotton T-Shirts and Pyjamas Set 3.5 (15) Limited time deal ₹597 ₹1,899 (69% off) FREE delivery Tue, 23 Jun See options Parent window ID44B3520FB43CDAF5133E0D5C87A1CFBC And User click on Add to cart button of the product titled "Kuchipoo". # stepDefinitions.AddCartSteps.AddCart(java.lang.String) Cart count: 0 Then examine the cart. # stepDefinitions.AddCartSteps.CInfo() 0 And delete all added products. # stepDefinitions.AddCartSteps.cDelete() And capture the Screenshot # stepDefinitions.Screenshot.takeScreenshot()

testcase.name: runScenario["Home page verification.", "Amazon Home page"](5)
testcase.system-out: Scenario: Home page verification. # src/test/resources/features/Home.feature:7 Given User is On Amazon login page. # stepDefinitions.LoginSteps.UseronLoginpage() When user enters a valid user email and pwd. # stepDefinitions.LoginSteps.validemail() Then click on login button. # stepDefinitions.LoginSteps.clkLogin() Given User is On Amazon home after succesful login. # stepDefinitions.HomePageSteps.homePage() When User notice on the top left corner. # stepDefinitions.HomePageSteps.LogoAtCorner() Logo is Present Then amazon logo is visible. # stepDefinitions.HomePageSteps.LogoVisible() When User notice the User profile. # stepDefinitions.HomePageSteps.UserProAtCorner() User profile is Present Then User can see User Profile. # stepDefinitions.HomePageSteps.userInfoVisible() Hello, Alekhya Account & Lists Then User can see Hello UserName. # stepDefinitions.HomePageSteps.userInfoTxtVisible() And capture the Screenshot # stepDefinitions.Screenshot.takeScreenshot()
